reasoning: Historically, cash assets of commercial banks generally grow corresponding with the growth of the broader economy and money supply. Although recent Quantitative Tightening (QT) has caused fluctuations and declines in bank reserves, it is broadly expected that QT will have ended or significantly tapered by late 2025. This would allow a resumption of a normal upward trend in cash assets. However, over a short ~3 month window (November 2025 to February 2026 timeframe), week-to-week volatility is significant, making it a coin toss with a slight upward bias. Therefore, a modest probability of 55% is assigned.
